meaning: adj.不受拘束的,不守规矩的,蛮横的,难驾驭的
examples: The man had remarkably black, unruly hair.
It's like trying to manage an unruly child.
Teddy ran thick fingers through his unruly thatch of hair.
An unruly, wild, wintertime break that picks up pretty much any north or west swell.
Day-Lewis looms as large as the derricks that dominate the unruly Central California landscape.
